reason: Both answers correctly identify the core issue (liability cap ~12 months fees, narrow-but-keep IP indemnity with standard carve-outs and remedy ladder, don't walk over negotiable boilerplate, emphasize asymmetric existential risk). Quality is close, but B adds a few genuinely useful, non-padding details A lacks: explicitly not indemnifying the customer's downstream customers, insisting on mutual liability exclusions, and a concrete actionable note about getting a startup lawyer to do a cheap fixed-fee redline. B's walk-away trigger is also more precisely calibrated than A's vaguer version. No factual errors in either.
